summary: I reviewed the full application diff for the dashboard feature (queries, broadcast service, controllers, Stimulus controllers, and views), cross-checking every referenced model enum and method (`Pipeline`/`Phase`/`StepRun`/`Worker`/`ManagerDecision` statuses, `status_badge` tone map, `AnswerQuestions::ANSWERABLE_STATUSES`) against the actual models, and confirmed the security-relevant paths are sound: all dashboard queries are correctly membership-scoped, the `answers` action stays authorized via `membership_scoped_phase`, the fleet `html_safe` output is pre-escaped via `content_tag`, and the sweep's "capture pipeline ids before `update_all`" ordering is correct. I found no correctness-breaking or security defects, so the verdict is **needs_work** on performance/quality grounds: two major findings — the recent-activity feed issues unbounded, history-growing reads that it trims to 15 only in Ruby (F2), and `Dashboard::Broadcast` recomputes the entire active-pipeline tree plus fleet health for every project member on every worker *progress* tick via `BroadcastCard` (F1) — plus two minors: the sweep broadcasts newly-stuck but not unstuck pipelines, leaving stale attention state (F3), and `manager_tick` fires the same full fan-out once per affected decision (F4). The verdict is written to `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/code-quality-critic/verdict.json`.
